ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
eosin: noun. A red, acidic dye commonly used in histological stains.
inquest: noun. A formal investigation, often held before a jury, especially one into the cause of a death
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
inset: verb. To set in; infix or implant.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
its: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Itsekiri.
nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nose: noun. A protuberance on the face housing the nostrils, which are used to breathe or smell.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
nous: noun. The mind or intellect, reason, both rational and emotional.
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
onset: noun. The initial phase of a disease or condition, in which symptoms first become apparent.
quest: noun. A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.
question: noun. A worded or expressed sentence, phrase, or only a word on its own, which asks for information, a reply, or a response; an
quiet: adjective. With little or no sound; free of disturbing noise.
quiets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of quiet
quint: noun. An interval of one fifth.
quinte: noun. The fifth defensive position, with the sword hand held at waist height, and the tip of the sword at knee height.
quit: adjective. Released from obligation, penalty, etc; free, clear, or rid.
quite: adverb. To the greatest extent or degree; completely, entirely.
quits: adjective. On equal monetary terms; neither owing or being owed.
quoin: noun. Any of the corner building blocks of a building, usually larger or more ornate than the surrounding blocks.
quoit: noun. A flat disc of metal or stone thrown at a target in the game of quoits.
quote: noun. A statement attributed to a person; a quotation.
sei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seri.
sen: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nanerige.
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
sequin: noun. Any of various small gold coins minted in Italy and Turkey.
sit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sino-Tibetan languages.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
snot: noun. Mucus, especially mucus from the nose.
snout: noun. The long, projecting nose, mouth, and jaw of a beast, as of pigs.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
sone: noun. a subjective unit of loudness for an average listener equal to the loudness of a 1000-hertz sound that has an intensity 40 decibels
sot: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Sotho.
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
squint: verb. To look with the eyes partly closed, as in bright sunlight, or as a threatening expression.
stein: noun. A beer mug, usually made of ceramic or glass.
steno: noun. a stenographer, someone whose job is to take dictation in shorthand
stone: noun. A hard earthen substance that can form rocks; especially, such substance when regarded as a building material.
stun: verb. To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.
suq: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Suri.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masadiit Itneg.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
toque: noun. A type of hat with no brim.
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
unite: verb. To bring together as one.
unites: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of unite
